title: NOC 62029 – Other Services Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 62029 is a versatile category covering supervisors in niche but essential service areas, including dry cleaning, parking, and specialized personal services. These TEER 2 professionals are vital for the operational diversity of Canadian cities. At GTR Immigration, we help these specialized supervisors find their unique path to PR, ensuring that their specific technical skills are correctly mapped to Canada’s economic needs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62029

duties: Coordinate and supervise the work of service staff in specialized areas.
Establish work schedules and procedures and ensure they are followed.
Train staff in specialized job duties and safety procedures.
Monitor performance and handle customer inquiries or complaints.
Requisition materials and supplies needed for operations.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Several years of experience in the specific service area are mandatory.
Specific technical knowledge of the industry (e.g., chemical usage in dry cleaning) is required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Continued urban development ensures a steady requirement for these specialized operational supervisors.
labour_market_demand: The demand for "Other" services supervisors is consistent across all major provinces. As Canada’s urban centers become more service-dense, the need for operational leads in specialized personal and business services continues to grow steadily.
Specialization: Become an expert in unique service-management niches.
Entrepreneurship: Excellent foundation for eventually opening your own service business.
Resilience: Many of these services are high-frequency, daily-need businesses.
pathways: Express Entry (CEC): The primary path for localized service supervisors.
Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Selective opportunities in provinces like Saskatchewan and Alberta.
